question_text: A gas is heated in a sealed container. What happens to the pressure of the gas and why?
correct_answer: The pressure increases because heating causes the gas particles to move faster and collide with the container walls more frequently and with greater force.
explanation: When you heat a gas in a sealed container, the particles gain energy and move faster. Faster-moving particles bump into the walls more often and hit them harder each time. Since pressure is caused by these collisions, more frequent and harder collisions mean higher pressure.
